ontology.foodex2_description: Shad and similar small pelagic fish, raw
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for gizzard shad (Selangat / Konosirus punctatus); A00GJ (shad and similar small pelagic fish) is the closest defensible parent code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept identified for gizzard shad as of 2026.

serving.description: 1 medium whole fish or fillet portion
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of raw gizzard shad is approximately 100 g, equivalent to one small whole fish or a single fillet portion as purchased from a wet market.

nutrient_source.title: Gizzard Shad (Selangat), Raw — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Gizzard shad (Selangat), raw (F03010018). All core macros and micronutrients are sourced from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: N/A — Glycemic index not applicable for raw fish with negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Gizzard shad (Selangat), raw contains only 0.4 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable for foods with negligible carbohydrate content. No GI value is assigned. Glycemic load per serving is effectively 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (160 mg/100 g) with high potassium (370 mg/100 g) and omega-3 fatty acids
hypertension.guidance: Raw gizzard shad is suitable for hypertensive individuals when prepared without added salt or high-sodium sauces; its favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio supports blood pressure regulation, and its omega-3 fatty acids improve endothelial function.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate (0.4 g/100 g) and high protein (19 g/100 g) with no sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: Gizzard shad is an excellent protein source for people with Type 2 Diabetes; its near-zero carbohydrate content produces no meaningful glycaemic response, and the high protein content supports satiety and slows gastric emptying — avoid battered or sweet-marinated preparations.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Oily fish providing omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA), moderate cholesterol (68 mg/100 g), and high protein
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: As a small oily fish, gizzard shad provides cardioprotective omega-3 fatty acids that reduce triglycerides and platelet aggregation; it should be prepared by steaming, grilling, or poaching rather than deep-frying to preserve its cardiovascular benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA) and calcium (190 mg/100 g)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Gizzard shad is beneficial for rheumatoid arthritis patients due to its omega-3 fatty acids that inhibit prostaglandin-mediated joint inflammation and its high calcium content (190 mg/100 g) that supports bone health; avoid deep-frying which generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that worsen joint inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Gizzard shad, known locally in Singapore and Malaysia as Selangat (Konosirus punctatus), is a small, oily marine fish with silvery scales, white-to-greyish flesh, and a soft, rich texture. It is commonly sold fresh at wet markets across Singapore, Malaysia, and the broader Southeast Asian region. In its raw state, gizzard shad provides 160 kcal per 100 g, with a high protein content of 19 g, moderate fat of 8.3 g (predominantly unsaturated, including omega-3 fatty acids EPA and DHA), and negligible carbohydrate of 0.4 g. It is an excellent source of calcium (190 mg/100 g), making it particularly valuable when consumed whole with its small bones. Potassium at 370 mg/100 g and low sodium at 160 mg/100 g give it a strongly favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio for blood pressure management. Clinically, gizzard shad is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (negligible glycaemic impact), hypertension (low sodium, high potassium, omega-3 support), and cardiovascular disease (cardioprotective omega-3 fatty acids). It is also beneficial for rheumatoid arthritis patients due to its anti-inflammatory omega-3 content. Gizzard shad is typically prepared by deep-frying, steaming, or braising in Singapore and Malaysian cuisine, though steaming or grilling is preferred for health-conscious preparation. It is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: Is ikan selangat high in omega-3?

confidence_reason: Core macros and micronutrients sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F03010018) at confidence 0.95; this is an exact match from the primary approved regional source for Singapore foods. Fatty acid breakdown (saturated, monounsaturated, polyunsaturated) is not reported in the HPB record and is set to -1.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these fields are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Gizzard shad (Selangat), raw (F03010018) as of 2026-04-16. sugar_g is set to 0.0 as confirmed zero per HPB record (no sugar reported for raw fish). dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as confirmed zero per HPB record. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.4 g/100 g); gi_category set to not_applicable and glycemic_index set to null per the zero-carbohydrate GI rule. The rheumatoid_arthritis entry in the input health_suitability had an incorrect suitable=false flag — this has been corrected to suitable=true in health_context as gizzard shad is beneficial for RA patients due to omega-3 and calcium content. FoodEx2 code A00GJ is used as closest_parent; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for Konosirus punctatus (gizzard shad / Selangat) as of 2026. foodex2_match_type corrected from analogous_estimate to closest_parent to comply with the allowed enumeration values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] required by G5.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all six hard gates and all eight Q criteria after three iterations. The two issues identified by the validator in iteration 2 have been resolved: (1) glycemic_index is correctly set to null for a negligible-carbohydrate fish, satisfying Q4; (2) foodex2_match_type is corrected to 'closest_parent' — a valid enumeration value — satisfying G5. Core macros are complete and sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (exact match, confidence 0.95). Mandatory Singaporean multilingual names are present. All -1 sentinel values are documented in notes. Ontology uses a defensible FoodOn exact match and FoodEx2 closest_parent with honest gap notes. Health context covers all three required conditions with specific clinical guidance. LLM description is comprehensive. Aliases are consistent between flat array and structured object. Final decision: PASS.
